video:video.video:title: Introducing Money Happens: A new AP audio series
video:video.video:description: Listen to Money Happens, a new audio series where the AP follows how people are dealing with debt, housing and rising costs and provides practical tips from financial experts

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 24-30 April 2026

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.

video:video.video:title: A converted church in rural Pennsylvania is becoming an incubator for Amish roots music
video:video.video:description: Ragamuffin Hall in McCoysville was the setting last weekend for a concert by two of the rising stars, Ben and Rose Stoltzfus.

video:video.video:title: Hospitals in southern Lebanon face uphill struggle as they treat the wounded and displaced
video:video.video:description: Hospitals in southern Lebanon are under immense strain and overwhelmed due to the dual pressures of war injuries and displacement. (AP Video: Bassam Hatoum

video:video.video:title: Convoy of oil trucks shows war-battered Syria selling itself as safe corridor amid regional conflict
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of oil-laden trucks, some arriving from Iraq, lined up along Syria's coastal road on Friday as they wait to unload into tankers at Baniyas port.

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame

video:video.video:title: Long DACA renewal wait times leave some 'Dreamers' without status, a job and fearing detainment
video:video.video:description: Renewal wait times for the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program that allows people who were brought to the U.S. as children to temporarily remain in the country and work have increased dramatically in the past year.

video:video.video:title: Ford is back with employee pricing for all as gas and car prices continue to climb
video:video.video:description: Ford Motor Co. is offering employee pricing to customers, as gas prices in the U.S. hit new multiyear highs and the cost of a new vehicle hovers near $50,000 (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Banksy confirms central London statue of man blinded by flag is his
video:video.video:description: A new sculpture in central London of a man striding off a plinth with his face blinded by a billowing flag was claimed Thursday by the elusive street artist Banksy, more than a day after it mysteriously appeared.

video:video.video:title: A citizen campaign returns iconic kiwi birds to New Zealand's capital after century-long absence
video:video.video:description: The kiwi, New Zealand’s sacred national bird, vanished from the hills around Wellington more than a century ago, but now the capital's residents are waging an improbable citizen campaign to return the endangered flightless birds to the city.

video:video.video:title: Zoo in New Orleans welcomes rare Hartmann’s mountain zebras for the first time
video:video.video:description: Hartmann’s mountain zebra are listed as a species vulnerable to extinction due to habitat loss from livestock farming, climate-induced drought, and competition for water.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court weakens the Voting Rights Act, hollowing out law that protects minority voting rights
video:video.video:description: Six decades ago, the Voting Rights Act became one of the most consequential laws in the nation’s history. On Wednesday, the U.S.

video:video.video:title: May Day protesters block roadway at SFO's international terminal
video:video.video:description: Protesters blocked a road outside the international terminal at San Francisco’s airport on Friday, leading to the street's closure for about two hours.

video:video.video:title: Learn the Eurovision dance moves
video:video.video:description: Eurovision 2026 contestants contestants teach the dance routines for their songs.

video:video.video:title: Body camera video shows moment house explodes as NYPD responds to call
video:video.video:description: A massive, fiery explosion at a house in Queens sent several New York City police officers flying through the air early Thursday morning as authorities were responding to a call of a man inside armed with a knife and the smell of gas.

video:video.video:title: May Day rally in New York targets Amazon
video:video.video:description: May Day demonstrators in New York speak out against Amazon, which they say has been pushing small business out of communities.

video:video.video:title: Current and former Mississippi lawmakers prepare for a new chapter in the fight for voting rights
video:video.video:description: Former Mississippi State Rep. Edward Blackmon Jr. and his son, State Sen. Bradford Blackmon, reflect on their generational fight for voting rights after a recent U.S.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Police in Istanbul detain hundreds trying to get to Taksim Square in May Day Demo
video:video.video:description: Police detained at least 15 protesters who were trying to reach Istanbul's Taksim Square Friday, the epicenter of landmark 2013 protests, in defiance of a government ban.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents’ dinner
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities said a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents’ dinner
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities said a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner and attempt to kill U.S.

video:video.video:title: Stabbing at Washington state high school wounds 6, including suspect, police say
video:video.video:description: A student at a Tacoma, Washington high school was booked on five counts of first-degree assault after four students and an adult security guard were wounded in a stabbing at the school Thursday, police said.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III's charity celebrates 50 years of helping young people find work with a gala in NYC
video:video.video:description: As King Charles III and Queen Camilla continued their first U.S. state visit since he became king, they highlighted The King’s Trust at a New York gala Wednesday in New York, as the charity expands its impact in more than two dozen countries.

video:video.video:title: Thousands rally for workers rights outside Phildelphia's City Hall
video:video.video:description: Thousands rally for workers rights outside Phildelphia's City Hall

video:video.video:title: EU sustainability rules push luxury fashion house Ferragamo to map leather origins
video:video.video:description: Luxury fashion brand Ferragamo is the first to provide figures on material traceability, particularly for leather.

video:video.video:title: Amtrak may ease gun restrictions on trains despite security concerns
video:video.video:description: Amtrak is considering allowing passengers to store guns in lockboxes on most trains, raising security concerns after an alleged attempt on President Donald Trump’s life involving a rail traveler.

video:video.video:title: It's a good start but uncertainty looms as Croatia braces for summer tourism season
video:video.video:description: With summer a few months away the streets of Croatia's old town of Dubrovnik are already teeming with tourists.

video:video.video:title: Eurovision contestants teach dance routines for their songs
video:video.video:description: Eurovision Song Contest hopefuls - including Austria's Cosmó, Germany's Sarah Engels, Ukraine's Leléka, Estonia's Vanilla Ninja, and Senhit with Boy George for San Marino - demonstrate their dance moves ahead of the annual event.

video:video.video:title: Russian drone attack in Ukraine's Ternopil city wounds 12, local officials say
video:video.video:description: The Ukrainian city of Ternopil was targeted by at least 36 drones on Friday wounding at least 12, the regional military administration said in a statement.

video:video.video:title: Experts warn of rising lead risks in Africa’s solar energy boom
video:video.video:description: Years after a toxic battery recycling plant shut down in Kenya’s coastal city of Mombasa, residents of Owino Uhuru village said they are still living with the scars of lead poisoning, a warning, experts say, of a growing public health risk as Africa’s clean energy boom drives a surge in battery use.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 1
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 1st: Prosecutors release surveillance video from first moment of Correspondents Dinner shooting; Louisiana Congressional primaries suspended; Congress faces War Powers Act deadline over Iran war; Stabbing attack in high school near Seattle.

video:video.video:title: Stanley Tucci and Emily Blunt receive stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Meryl Streep and Matt Damon were among the stars singing Tucci and Blunt's praises at the ceremony. The two "Devil Wears Prada" actors are also in-laws since Tucci married Blunt's sister, Felicity in 2012.

video:video.video:title: Wild animals are making their homes next to ours. Experts say it’s only natural
video:video.video:description: Spring is when wildlife nest or bear young, often close to humans. Experts say homeowners should enjoy their new neighbors but leave nests and young animals alone.

video:video.video:title: Isabel Allende gives 'The House of the Spirits' TV adaptation her stamp of approval
video:video.video:description: Best-selling author Isabel Allende has given Prime Video's "The House of the Spirits" her stamp of approval.

video:video.video:title: Your climate impact doesn't end when you die. More people are considering 'greener' death options
video:video.video:description: As interest in alternative forms of burial has risen in recent years, some have turned to green, or conservation burials, to reduce the environmental impact of their passing, such as at the Prairie Creek Conservation Cemetery outside Gainesville, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Workers around the world demonstrate on May Day
video:video.video:description: People across the world kicked off May Day with large marches and protests as many workers grapple with rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing power tied to the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Indiana Black voters, candidates raise concerns about potential new redistricting efforts
video:video.video:description: An effort pushed by U.S. President Donald Trump last November to redraw Indiana’s congressional map failed.

video:video.video:title: Chilean puppet show '31 Minutos' draws massive crowd in Mexico City
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans gathered Thursday evening in Mexico City's central square to watch a free live performance of the Chilean television show "31 Minutos" to mark Children’s Day.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration claims its war in Iran ended with April ceasefire, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is arguing that the war in Iran has already ended because of the ceasefire that began in April, an interpretation that would allow the White House to avoid a federal law requirement for congressional approval of military action that extends beyond 60 days.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures attack on Nun near Jerusalem’s old city
video:video.video:description: Israeli police said Friday that they arrested a 36-year-old caught on video attacking a nun in the latest incident targeting Christians near Jerusalem's Old City.

video:video.video:title: Shakira fans gather in Rio ahead of open-air performance on Copacabana Beach
video:video.video:description: The singer is performing a massive open-air concert along the waterfront in the city's iconic Copacabana Beach next Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Workers around the world demonstrate on May Day
video:video.video:description: People across the world kicked off May Day with large marches and protests as many workers grapple with rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing power tied to the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Habitat for Humanity is developing a new Atlanta community with help from the Carters' initiative
video:video.video:description: In only five days, nearly 1,000 volunteers with Habitat for Humanity will complete 24 affordable housing units in Atlanta's Sylvan Hills neighborhood.

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt with police as people in Manila march to the US Embassy on May Day
video:video.video:description: Workers in the Philippines clashed with police near the US Embassy in the capital Manila on Friday during their Labor Day protest. (AP video by Joeal Calupitan)

video:video.video:title: Demos held in Latin American countries to mark Labor Day
video:video.video:description: Demonstrators turned out in Venezuela, Cuba, Ecuador and Argentina. In Mexico City, sex workers held a protest demanding dignity and protections.

video:video.video:title: A rich pour of heritage: Yemeni coffeehouses rise across the US
video:video.video:description: Yemeni coffeehouses are rapidly expanding across the U.S., blending centuries-old coffee traditions with modern café culture and serving as community-focused social spaces.

video:video.video:title: Trump jokes that US Navy will take on Cuba on the way home from Iran
video:video.video:description: “Cuba’s got problems,” Trump said in his speech before the non-profit Forum Club of the Palm Beaches at the Raymond F. Kravis Center for the Performing Arts.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran wants to make a deal but he's 'not satisfied with it'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday he’s “not satisfied” with Iran’s latest proposal to end the war, citing “disjointed” leadership in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Russia launches new Soyuz-5 rocket in first test flight
video:video.video:description: A new Russian medium-class launch vehicle Soyuz-5 was launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome overnight Thursday as part of its first flight tests, Russia’s state space corporation Roscosmos said.

video:video.video:title: Returnees to southern Lebanon face difficult choices as they survey their flattened homes
video:video.video:description: Residents of southern Lebanon on Thursday surveyed damaged homes and buildings flattened to the ground.

video:video.video:title: Winnie Harlow begins Met Gala planning early, but keeps her look under wraps
video:video.video:description: Winnie Harlow kept her Met Gala look under wraps, offering no details, but said she begins thinking about the event long before invitations arrive. (May 1)
